title: glebionis coronaria
intro: Glebionis coronaria, also known as the Corn Marigold, is an annual or perennial plant in the daisy family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high levels of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Glebionis coronaria include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional cooking and as a natural dye.
